chunks.heading: Short answer: Who is Aseel?
chunks.text:
[about — Short answer: Who is Aseel?]
Aseel Herzallah is a junior software engineer and Computer Science graduate from Ben-Gurion University of the Negev. She builds across full-stack web development, AI-powered applications, backend systems, and lower-level systems programming.

Her strongest profile is not limited to one narrow layer of the stack. She has built production-style React/Node applications, integrated LLM features into real products, worked with Python AI tooling, and completed hands-on systems projects in C, C++, and Java.

chunks.heading: 30-second recruiter summary
chunks.text:
[about — 30-second recruiter summary]
Aseel is a B.Sc. Computer Science graduate with practical project experience in full-stack development, generative AI, machine learning, networking, concurrency, and operating systems. She is strongest when she owns a feature end to end: understanding the problem, designing the architecture, implementing the backend, building the UI, debugging edge cases, and deploying the result.

Her work includes a full MERN Twitter/X-style social platform with authentication, feeds, DMs, notifications, profile management, Cloudinary uploads, and an in-app AI assistant; AI/automation projects using OpenAI, LangChain, Groq, FastAPI, Streamlit, and Notion; a CNN-based camera pose estimation project; and BGU systems projects involving xv6, system calls, shared memory, synchronization, sockets, Java multithreading, and C++ memory management.

chunks.heading: What kind of engineer is Aseel?
chunks.text:
[about — What kind of engineer is Aseel?]
Aseel is a product-minded junior engineer with strong CS fundamentals. She is comfortable moving between user-facing features, backend APIs, AI integrations, and systems-level debugging.

She cares about:
- building software that works outside a local demo
- understanding how systems behave under the hood
- writing practical, maintainable code
- debugging patiently and systematically
- learning new tools by building with them
- explaining technical ideas clearly to others

chunks.heading: What makes Aseel's background different?
chunks.text:
[about — What makes Aseel's background different?]
Aseel combines three layers that are valuable for a junior software engineer:

1. **Full-stack product building**  
   She has built complete web applications with React, TypeScript/JavaScript, Node.js, Express, MongoDB, REST APIs, authentication, deployment, and responsive UI work.

2. **AI and automation experience**  
   She has built LLM-powered tools, chatbots, RAG-style workflows, AI scoring pipelines, and integrations using tools such as OpenAI, LangChain, Groq, FastAPI, Streamlit, and Notion.

3. **Systems foundation**  
   Her BGU coursework and projects include xv6 kernel work, C/C++, Java multithreading, sockets, synchronization, virtual memory concepts, process management, and client-server architecture.

This gives her a practical engineering range: she can build a feature, understand the infrastructure behind it, and reason about correctness, performance, and reliability.

chunks.heading: What is Aseel looking for?
chunks.text:
[about — What is Aseel looking for?]
Aseel is looking for her first full-time junior software engineering role. She is especially interested in:
- full-stack software engineering
- backend development
- AI software engineering
- LLM-integrated products
- systems-oriented software roles
- teams building real products with modern engineering practices

chunks.heading: Strengths
chunks.text:
[about — Strengths]
Aseel's strengths include:
- end-to-end ownership
- fast learning through hands-on projects
- strong debugging mindset
- solid CS fundamentals
- comfort with both high-level product work and lower-level systems topics
- communication and mentoring experience
- multilingual collaboration in Arabic, Hebrew, and English

chunks.heading: Personality as a teammate
chunks.text:
[about — Personality as a teammate]
Aseel is curious, persistent, and practical. She likes understanding why things work, not only how to make them work once. She is comfortable asking questions, learning from feedback, and improving through iteration.

Because she has mentored CS and software engineering students, she is also used to explaining complex ideas clearly and helping others build confidence with difficult technical material.

chunks.heading: B.Sc. in Computer Science
chunks.text:
[education — B.Sc. in Computer Science]
**Institution:** Ben-Gurion University of the Negev  
**Location:** Be'er Sheva  
**Years:** 2022–2026  
**Degree:** Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

chunks.heading: Academic focus
chunks.text:
[education — Academic focus]
Aseel's academic background gave her a strong foundation in software engineering and computer science fundamentals. Her coursework and projects covered both theoretical and practical topics, including algorithms, operating systems, programming languages, concurrency, object-oriented programming, systems programming, networking, and AI/ML-related work.

chunks.heading: Operating systems
chunks.text:
[education — Operating systems]
Aseel worked with the xv6-riscv teaching operating system and implemented kernel-level functionality. This included system calls, process management, scheduling-related work, shared memory, synchronization, and debugging under low-level environments such as QEMU/GDB.

Important concepts:
- system calls
- user-to-kernel argument handling
- process creation and waiting
- shared memory mapping
- page table manipulation
- reference counting
- synchronization primitives
- Peterson locks
- tournament tree synchronization
- sleep/wakeup behavior
- race-condition debugging

chunks.heading: Systems programming
chunks.text:
[education — Systems programming]
Aseel completed systems programming projects in Java and C++. These projects strengthened her understanding of runtime behavior, memory management, sockets, concurrency, object-oriented design, and client-server architecture.

Important concepts:
- Java multithreading
- thread-safe state management
- TCP sockets
- binary protocols
- server-client architecture
- C++ Rule of 5
- RAII and memory ownership
- object-oriented design
- practical debugging

Aseel has mentored and tutored CS and software engineering students from BGU and SCE. Her mentoring experience includes helping students with courses such as Data Structures, Design of Algorithms, Systems Programming Languages, Algebra, and related CS fundamentals.

This is important because it shows not only technical ability, but also communication, patience, and the ability to explain difficult concepts clearly.

chunks.text:

Aseel's degree trained her to think beyond surface-level implementation. She learned to reason about correctness, complexity, memory, concurrency, and system behavior. This foundation supports her work in full-stack development and AI applications because she understands both product-level requirements and underlying engineering tradeoffs.

chunks.text:

She also has systems-level backend foundations through Java socket programming, TCP client-server work, and xv6 operating-system projects.

chunks.heading: Does Aseel have frontend experience?
chunks.text:

Yes. Aseel works with React, JavaScript, TypeScript, HTML/CSS, responsive UI, Vite, Tailwind CSS, component architecture, state management, TanStack Query, and modern frontend routing. Her Twitter clone is mobile-first and includes light/dark/system theme support.

chunks.heading: Does Aseel have AI experience?
chunks.text:

Yes. Aseel has practical AI experience with LLM integrations, LangChain, Groq, OpenAI, Streamlit, RAG concepts, AI agents, and automation workflows. She also has ML/computer vision experience through a CNN-based camera pose estimation project using TensorFlow/Keras and synthetic 3D renderings.

chunks.heading: What is Aseel's camera pose estimation project?
chunks.text:
[faq — What is Aseel's camera pose estimation project?]
It is an AI/ML project where Aseel generated synthetic 3D model renderings and trained a CNN to predict camera orientation. The project involved Python, TensorFlow/Keras, CNNs, computer vision, 3D rendering tools such as PyVista and Open3D, image preprocessing, model training, and evaluation. The reported validation accuracy was 97%.

Her work includes system calls, process management, shared memory, synchronization, Peterson locks, tournament trees, QEMU/GDB debugging, Java multithreading, TCP sockets, binary protocols, and C++ memory management.

chunks.heading: TFTP Server and Client
chunks.text:
[projects — TFTP Server and Client]
A Java TCP client-server system using a binary protocol, thread-per-client architecture, login, file upload/download, directory listing, delete operations, and broadcast notifications for file changes.

Technologies:
Java, TCP, sockets, multithreading, Maven, networking, binary protocols.

chunks.text:
[projects — Set Card Game]
A multithreaded Java Set card game with human and AI players, parallel legal-set computation, synchronized game-state management, and thread-safe real-time gameplay.

Technologies:
Java, multithreading, concurrency, synchronization, OOP, thread-safe design.

chunks.heading: Food Warehouse Management System
chunks.text:
[projects — Food Warehouse Management System]
A C++ warehouse simulation with volunteer and customer roles, order lifecycle management, command system, backup/restore, and careful memory management using Rule of 5 design.

Technologies:
C++, OOP, Rule of 5, memory management, data structures.

chunks.heading: Best recruiter answer
chunks.text:
[projects — Best recruiter answer]
The SPL projects show Aseel's ability to build software with concurrency, sockets, and memory-management constraints, not only high-level web applications.

chunks.heading: Short description
chunks.text:
[projects — Short description]
A security-focused educational project combining a memory game interface with simulated Remote Access Trojan behavior and social engineering analysis.

chunks.heading: Why this project matters
chunks.text:
[projects — Why this project matters]
This project shows Aseel's interest in security awareness and understanding how malicious tooling can work in order to reason defensively.

chunks.heading: Main features
chunks.text:
[projects — Main features]
- memory game as a front-end layer
- simulated RAT behavior
- TCP command channel concepts
- XOR-encrypted command communication
- remote shell execution concepts
- webcam snapshot concept using OpenCV
- social engineering scenario analysis
- PyInstaller packaging exploration

chunks.heading: Technologies
chunks.text:
[projects — Technologies]
Python, Pygame, TCP, OpenCV, PyInstaller, security concepts, social engineering analysis.

chunks.heading: Important note
chunks.text:
[projects — Important note]
This project should be described as educational and defensive-awareness oriented. It is not intended as a malicious tool.

chunks.heading: Professional summary
chunks.text:
[resume — Professional summary]
Aseel Herzallah is a junior software engineer with strong foundations in software development, problem solving, full-stack web development, AI-powered applications, backend systems, and operating systems.

She has hands-on experience with Java, Python, C/C++, JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, Express, MongoDB, REST APIs, and modern AI tooling. Her project experience spans full-stack applications, LLM integrations, machine learning/computer vision, networking, concurrency, and kernel-level operating systems work.

She is seeking an entry-level software engineering role where she can contribute, learn, and grow.
